identity.short_name: Reciprocal-product square filter -> Hilbert-series degree count

subdomain(s): integer polynomials and squares in Z[x]
gcd factor splitting in polynomial rings
Chevalley restriction and Weyl group invariants
graded dimension / Hilbert series coefficient extraction

problem_spec.summary: Composite (CHAIN) family: compute bridge quantity T from component A (Count non-palindromic bounded quartics with reciprocal product a square); then use that T as a required parameter in component B (SL4 adjoint invariant polynomials (degree 50)) to determine the final output (reduced mod 1000). Instance summary: Impose that a quadratic's reciprocal product is a perfect square (excluding palindromic/anti-palindromic degeneracies) to obtain a count T, then use T to set a Hilbert-series coefficient problem for an invariant ring with degrees 2,3,4; output mod 1000.
givens: Invariant ring degrees are 2,3,4; U counts solutions to 2i+3j+4k=10+T.

parameter(s).name: square_condition
parameter(s).purpose: forces even multiplicities of irreducible factors in P \cdot P*

parameter(s).name: nonpalindromic_filter
parameter(s).purpose: excludes the trivial palindromic branch (a=c) where P \cdot P* is automatically a square

parameter(s).name: polynomial_degree_d
parameter(s).purpose: selects the graded piece of the invariant polynomial ring to be counted

parameter(s).name: lie_type_fixed
parameter(s).purpose: fixes the Weyl group (S_4) and fundamental invariant degrees (2,3,4) for the adjoint action

parameter(s).name: T_bridge
parameter(s).domain: derived integer computed from component A invariants
parameter(s).purpose: interface quantity produced by component A and consumed by component B (non-stapled coupling).

invariant(s).name: square_coefficient_constraints
invariant(s).statement: If R_{a,b}=(ux^2+vx+w)^2, then b=u^2=w^2 and coefficient matching forces strong constraints on a.

invariant(s).name: palindromic_implies_square
invariant(s).statement: If P*=P (equivalently c=a), then P \cdot P* = P^2 is a square in Z[x].

invariant(s).name: gcd_with_reciprocal_self_reciprocal
invariant(s).statement: D=gcd(P,P*) satisfies D*=±D; choosing D monic makes D*=D (self-reciprocal).

invariant(s).name: degree_2_gcd_forces_palindromic
invariant(s).statement: If deg gcd(P,P*)=2, then both the gcd and the remaining quadratic factor have constant term 1 and are self-reciprocal, forcing P*=P (palindromic).

invariant(s).name: coprime_product_square_forcing
invariant(s).statement: If gcd(P,P*)=1 and PP* is a square in Z[x], then P is a square in Z[x] up to a unit; monicity forces P=Q^2 with QZ[x].

invariant(s).name: odd_degree_not_square
invariant(s).statement: A nonzero square polynomial in Z[x] has even degree; in particular, no cubic in Z[x] is a square.

invariant(s).name: self_duality_of_adjoint
invariant(s).statement: V^*  V as G-modules via the nondegenerate Killing form, so dimensions of invariant subspaces in Sym^d(V^*) and Sym^d(V) agree.

invariant(s).name: chevalley_restriction_for_adjoint
invariant(s).statement: The invariant ring (Sym(V^*))^G is isomorphic (as a graded algebra) to (Sym(h^*))^W where h is a Cartan subalgebra and W is the Weyl group of G.

invariant(s).name: generator_degrees_for_sl4
invariant(s).statement: For sl_4 (type A_3), (Sym(V^*))^G is a polynomial algebra on three homogeneous generators of degrees 2,3,4, corresponding to the elementary symmetric polynomials e2,e3,e4 in eigenvalues under the trace-zero constraint.

invariant(s).name: graded_dimension_as_monomial_count
invariant(s).statement: If (Sym(V^*))^G  C[f2,f3,f4] with deg(f2)=2, deg(f3)=3, deg(f4)=4, then dim (Sym^d(V^*))^G = #{(a,b,c) in Z_{>=0}^3 : 2a+3b+4c=d}.

invariant(s).name: bridge_interface_invariant
invariant(s).statement: The composite is coupled by the interface variable T: component A uniquely determines T, and component B is defined/solved only after substituting this specific T into its parameter slot.

essential_assumption(s): Square root is taken in Z[x]; strict non-(anti-)palindromic rule removes degenerate always-square cases.
